key_finding: Best strategy: S8_accel / T2_roll_p75 / P3_long_short_counter / L3. OOS Sharpe 1.11 vs 0.7437 B&H. Max drawdown -13.5% vs -13.3% B&H.
mechanism: Rising industrial production signals economic expansion and improved corporate earnings. During expansions, investors rotate away from defensive consumer staples (XLP) toward growth sectors, causing XLP to underperform. During contractions (falling IP), defensive sectors outperform as investors seek capital preservation.
caveats: INDPRO is released with ~6-week publication lag — real-time trading must account for delay
COVID-19 April 2020 outlier (-12.7% MoM) may distort regime estimates
Consumer staples are defensive but not perfectly countercyclical — earnings quality matters
Monthly frequency limits signal granularity for daily strategies
XLP sample starts 1998 — shorter history than INDPRO (1919)
